title: Neverwinter

notes:
Steps:
1. Install POP!_OS 19.04
2. check> Terminal: "nvidia-smi"
Terminal> ubuntu-drivers devices | grep "nvidia-driver"
-Remove Old Nvidia Driver
sudo apt-get purge nvidia*
Install Latest Nvidia Driver on Ubuntu
sudo add-apt-repository ppa:graphics-drivers/ppa
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
ubuntu-drivers devices | grep "nvidia-driver"
- Install the driver from there (sudo apt install nvidia-driver-430) or newer version available.
- Update the initrd> Terminal> sudo update-initramfs -u
- Reboot
After Reboot
Terminal> lsmod | grep nvidia
3. nomodeset
Terminal> sudo nano /etc/default/grub
Change> GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=”quiet splash”
to> GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=”quiet splash nomodeset”
Save (Ctrl+S) then (Ctrl+X= Exit)
-Terminal> sudo update-grub2
-Restart
4. Change Mousepoll
Mouse Neverwinter > 1000Hz= 1 / 500Hz= 2 / 250Hz= 4 / 125Hz= 8
Terminal> cat /sys/module/usbhid/parameters/mousepoll > If 0 then change it with:
Terminal> sudo su - -c 'echo "options usbhid mousepoll=2" > /etc/modprobe.d/usbhid.conf' (New mouse models use 500Hz or 1000Hz pollrate)
Terminal> sudo modprobe -r usbhid && sudo modprobe usbhid mousepoll=2
Terminal> sudo update-grub
Terminal> sudo update-initramfs -u
Terminal> 
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
5.Install: Terminal>
sudo apt install gnome-tweak-tool 
-Select Flat Acceleration Profile in Keyboard & Mouse tab
sudo apt install ubuntu-restricted-extras
sudo apt install steam (Click on it and let it Update Steam)
sudo add-apt-repository ppa:samoilov-lex/gamemode
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt install gamemode
Use Steam: Library right click on Neverwinter> Properties>Set Launch Options:
Copy-Paste> gamemoderun %command%
6. Start Neverwinter (Download Proton and Neverwinter Launcher)
Start the Game and before logging in; go to options and uncheck "On-demand patching" then check "Force Verify"> Save and log in.
While downloading the Game check>
Terminal> gamemoded -s
 and look if Game Mode is Active or not (Example: 
~$ gamemoded -s
gamemode is active
7.Open Nvidia X Server Settings>
-In X Server Display Configuration check your resolution and refresh rate.
-X Server XVideo Settings> Sync to this display device (Check your Monitor or TV)
-OpenGL Settings> Un-check Sync to VBlank and Allow Flipping (No tearing or sttutering) Choose between quality or performance> move the bar.
-Antialiasing Settings> Use Application Settings (Un-check "Enable FXAA" and "Override Application Setting"
-Powermizer> Preferred Mode> Maximum Performance
-Go to nvidia-settings Configuration and save your current Configuration /home
8. Start the game and wait like 5 minutes to load it for the first time.
Change your settings in-game (Check V-Sync in Game and you can use Low shadows and FXAA, this way the GTX 960 in my case will work smoothly)
9. Save your settings and Interface.
10. Enjoy the Game.
Note: If you have a gaming mouse save your configuration into your mouse using windows or download piper and make your adjustments)
Glad to help! :D
title: Neverwinter

notes:
Getting minimum of 70 fps using multi-rendering option. Everything on max settings with 4K resolution. You can fix the game stutter with the launch options below 

Steam Launch Options:
mesa_glthread=true PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 DXVK_HUD=fps %command%

notes: Proton 4.11-7 Disables the mouse input causing an inability to click on anything in the game and therefore makes the game unplayable. By forcing proton 4.2-9 everything works as normal again and the game is playable. 4.11-7 breaks neverwinter online.
title: Neverwinter

notes: I am confirming that mouse doesn't work with Proton 4.11-7 . To play the game: Right Click -> Properties -> Check the "Force the use of a specific Steam Play compatibility tool" -> Select "Proton 4.2-9 from the drop menu"
